title: Maintenance and inactivation of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) as feeder cells for human pluripotent stem cell culture
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8orp6","text":"This collection describes the maintenance and inactivation of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) as feeder cells for human pluripotent stem cell (hPSC) culture.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"17hl4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"14ju9","text":"Collection overview","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5gsf5","text":"Thawing of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) for hPSC cultures","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5c5f","text":"Expansion of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) for hPSC cultures","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gltd","text":"Freezing of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) for hPSC cultures","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8orjp","text":"Harvesting and irradiation of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) for hPSC cultures","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4r5nl","text":"Mitomycin C inactivation of mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) for hPSC cultures","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fuujg","text":"Preparation of mouse embryonic fibroblast (MEF) feeder plates for hPSC cultures","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4rqpj","text":"    A. Starting with frozen irradiated or Mitomycin C inactivated MEFs (optional)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9e22g","text":"    B. Starting with fresh irradiated or Mitomycin C inactivated MEFs","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4fr1g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ls73","text":"General notes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a5kc9","text":"1. Throughout these protocols, the term hPSC is used to collectively refer to both hiPSCs and hESCs. All described procedures have been tested and work equally well for hiPSCs and hESCs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qljp","text":"2. Either fresh or frozen stocks of irradiated or Mitomycin C inactivated MEFs can be used to prepare hPSC feeder cells. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7lis","text":"3. The indicated MEF densities are recommended starting densities and might have to be adjusted for each hPSC line and hPSC media formulation (KSR, serum-free versus serum-containing media). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eb68a","text":"4.
